model_scope: RED country-year panel, observed RED countries, 1900-2014, global SILSO annualized solar exposure
identification_warning: Because SILSO solar activity is global and varies only by year, a country-year panel with year fixed effects cannot estimate a separate solar coefficient. These diagnostics use country fixed effects and polynomial time trends, so unmeasured year-level confounding remains possible.

event_within_country_fe_trend_year_clust.note: country fixed effects via within transform; standard errors clustered by year because solar exposure is common across countries in each year

event_history.definition: Onset is approximated from RED annual-by-country rows as a country-year with any RED episode after the same country had no RED event in the prior year. Country-years already active in the prior year are excluded from the risk set.

geometry_exposure.note: two-way fixed effects via country and year demeaning; exposure is solar_radiation_geomagnetic_exposure; controls are standardized before demeaning

nasa_power_exposure.note: two-way fixed effects via country and year demeaning; exposure is nasa_power_solar_geomagnetic_exposure; controls are standardized before demeaning

vulnerable_country_test.definition: Vulnerable test country-years are those above the first-half p75 vulnerability score: low V-Dem electoral democracy, low V-Dem civil-society participation, weak GDP growth, high inflation, and low food production.

geometry_exposure.note: Ridge-logistic out-of-sample onset screen with country dummies and controls. Lower Brier/log-loss is better; higher AUC is better. This is predictive validation, not causal identification.

nasa_power_exposure.note: insufficient train/test onset events for predictive screen

heterogeneity.scope: Subtype-specific onset screens use RED goal/form/outcome fields and the same at-risk definition. They are exploratory multiple-comparison diagnostics, not confirmatory subgroup claims.

country_year_controls.control_scope: pre-specified country-year political/economic controls merged by ISO3 and year
country_year_controls.controlled_model_note: Controls are included inside the two-way fixed-effect exposure model after standardization. This is still observational and loses years/countries where control data are missing. Out-of-sample prediction uses the denser V-Dem/WDI political-economy controls and reports the conflict-control FE model separately because WDI battle-death coverage is sparse.

identified_exposure.name: country-specific solar radiation geometry x geomagnetic coupling x SILSO exposure

nasa_power_exposure.name: NASA POWER ALLSKY_SFC_SW_DWN x geomagnetic coupling x SILSO exposure

exploratory_strengthening_tests.scope: Exploratory screens for stronger versions of the solar hypothesis: delayed effects, vulnerability interactions, exposure quantiles, and country-year circular-shift placebo timing. These are not confirmatory unless preregistered and validated out-of-sample.

geometry_exposure.note: two-way fixed effects via country and year demeaning; exposure is solar_radiation_geomagnetic_exposure_x_control_vdem_electoral_democracy; controls are standardized before demeaning
geometry_exposure.interpretation: Coefficient is the two-way-FE association for solar exposure multiplied by the standardized country-year control. This screens whether the solar association is conditional on political-economic vulnerability.

geographic_exposure.scope: Geographic exposure refinement: compare high versus lower geomagnetic-coupling country-years, test an exposure x high-sensitivity interaction, and test an auroral-oval proximity exposure proxy.

onset.note: two-way fixed effects via country and year demeaning; exposure is solar_auroral_proximity_exposure; controls are standardized before demeaning

geographic_exposure.note: Magnetic latitude and auroral proximity are coarse centroid-based proxies; they improve geographic specificity but are not full IGRF/WMM or storm-time auroral boundary modeling.

non_estimable_model.reason: Solar activity is identical for every country in a given year, so year fixed effects absorb all solar variation.
